text.title: nginx Reverse Proxy: Security Headers and Rate Limiting
text.summary: Security headers (HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy), rate limiting zones for API endpoints, and the snippets pattern for DRY configuration across multiple virtual hosts.

section.title: Security Headers
section.p: Apply via a snippet included in every server block. Do not set per-location unless a site needs CSP overrides.

rule.statement: Set HSTS max-age to at least 63072000 (2 years) for production; do not add preload until all subdomains support HTTPS.
rule.rationale: HSTS with preload is irreversible for months. A subdomain that loses its certificate will become inaccessible to browsers that cached the preload entry.

antipattern.description: Setting Content-Security-Policy in the global snippet
antipattern.reason: CSP requires per-site values (each app has different allowed sources). A global CSP either blocks functionality or is too permissive to matter.

section.title: Rate Limiting Zones
section.p: Define zones in http context (conf.d/rate-limiting.conf). Reference with limit_req in location blocks.

rule.statement: Use api_auth zone (3r/m) for login and password-reset endpoints to prevent brute force.
rule.rationale: General API rate limiting (10r/s) is too permissive for auth endpoints. A bot can attempt 600 passwords per minute before triggering it.

section.title: Snippets Pattern
section.p: Store reusable configuration fragments in /etc/nginx/snippets/ and include them with a single line.

list.item: proxy-params.conf — proxy headers for every proxied location
websocket.conf — HTTP/1.1 upgrade headers and 24h timeouts
security-headers.conf — HSTS and other response security headers
cloudflare-realip.conf — set_real_ip_from for all Cloudflare IP ranges
